info.description:
REST API for authenticated Linux users to manage Containerlab deployments, topology files, node access, captures, and host networking tools.

Most endpoints under /api/v1 require a JWT from POST /login. Send it as: Authorization: Bearer <token>.
info.title: Containerlab API Server

delete.description: Terminates all capture VNC sessions owned by the authenticated user. Superusers terminate all sessions.
Terminates a capture VNC session and stops the corresponding Wireshark container.

get.description: Returns whether a capture VNC session is ready and its proxied URL path.
Proxies noVNC HTTP assets for a capture session. WebSocket upgrades on this path are used by noVNC but are not represented in Swagger.

parameters.description: Proxy subpath under the noVNC root
parameters.name: proxyPath

get.description:
Streams containerlab events in real time as NDJSON (one JSON object per line).

**Notes**
- The response stays open until the client disconnects.

**Examples**
NDJSON (one JSON object per line):
```json
{"time":1706918400,"type":"container","action":"start","attributes":{"name":"clab-mylab-srl1","lab":"mylab","clab-node-name":"srl1","clab-node-kind":"nokia_srlinux"}}
{"time":1706918405,"type":"container","action":"start","attributes":{"name":"clab-mylab-srl2","lab":"mylab","clab-node-name":"srl2","clab-node-kind":"nokia_srlinux"}}
```


Interface stats (interfaceStats=true):
```json
{"time":1706918410,"type":"interface-stats","action":"stats","attributes":{"name":"clab-mylab-srl1","lab":"mylab","interface":"e1-1","rx_bytes":123456,"tx_bytes":654321}}
```

parameters.description: Include initial snapshot events when the stream starts.
parameters.name: initialState

parameters.description: Interval for interface stats collection (e.g., 10s). Requires interfaceStats=true.
parameters.name: interfaceStatsInterval

200.description: Event stream - NDJSON (one JSON object per line)

post.description:
Generates a containerlab topology from CLOS definitions and can optionally deploy it for the authenticated user.

**Notes**
- Deployment is denied if a lab with the target name already exists.
- The `images` and `licenses` fields map node kind to image or license path (e.g., {"nokia_srlinux":"ghcr.io/..."}).
- When `deploy=true`, the topology is saved to the user's `~/.clab/<labName>/` directory and `outputFile` is ignored.
- When `deploy=false` and `outputFile` is empty, YAML is returned in the response.
- When `deploy=false` and `outputFile` is set, the file is saved to that path on the server (requires API server write permissions).

parameters.description: Topology generation parameters. The 'images' field maps kind to image path.
parameters.name: generate_request

500.description: Internal server error or clab execution failed

get.description: Returns detailed CPU, memory, and disk metrics for the API server. Requires authentication.

Returns details for all running labs.

**Notes**
- Results are filtered by owner unless the caller is a superuser.

Deploys a containerlab topology.

**Notes**
- The request body must include either `topologyContent` or `topologySourceUrl` (not both).

put.description:
Redeploys a lab by name.

**Notes**
- This operation destroys the lab and then deploys it again.
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

parameters.description: Name of the lab to redeploy
parameters.name: labName

parameters.description: Remove containerlab lab artifacts during destroy phase
parameters.name: cleanup

parameters.description: Custom Go template file for topology data export
parameters.name: exportTemplate

parameters.description: Skip setting extended ACLs on lab directory
parameters.name: skipLabdirAcl

parameters.description: Stream lifecycle output as NDJSON events
parameters.name: stream

parameters.description: Include captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response
parameters.name: includeLogs

delete.description:
Destroys a lab by name after verifying ownership.

**Notes**
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

parameters.description: Remove containerlab lab artifacts after destroy

parameters.description: Stream lifecycle output as NDJSON events

parameters.description: Include captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response
parameters.name: includeLogs

post.description: Builds packetflix URI(s) for the provided lab interface targets (non-VNC capture mode).
Creates one or more Wireshark noVNC capture sessions for the specified lab interface targets.

Deploys an on-disk topology from the authenticated user's lab directory.

**Notes**
- `path` defaults to `<labName>.clab.yml` when omitted.
- `stream=true` returns `application/x-ndjson` lifecycle events.
- `includeLogs=true` includes captured lifecycle logs in the JSON response.

parameters.description: Relative topology file path inside lab directory (defaults to <labName>.clab.yml)
parameters.name: path

parameters.description: Custom Go template file for topology data export
parameters.name: exportTemplate

parameters.description: Stream lifecycle output as NDJSON events

parameters.description: Execute only on this specific node
parameters.name: nodeFilter

post.description: Runs an fcli command against the selected lab topology.
Executes GoTTY share action (attach, detach, reattach) for a lab.
Generates a draw.io diagram from a lab topology and returns the generated XML content.

get.description: Returns interface details for nodes in a lab.

parameters.description: Filter interfaces for a specific node
parameters.name: node

get.description: Returns exposed host ports for a node suitable for opening in a browser.

Returns logs for a lab node.

**Notes**
- When `follow=true`, the response streams as NDJSON (one JSON object per line) until the client disconnects or the 30-minute timeout.

parameters.description: Number of lines to show from the end of logs (default all). Use an integer or 'all'.
parameters.name: tail

parameters.description: Follow log output (stream logs as NDJSON). Note: In Swagger UI, streaming may not display correctly.
parameters.name: follow

200.description: Container logs (follow=false). When follow=true, response is NDJSON stream of LogLine objects.

400.description: Invalid input (lab name, node filter, etc.)

post.description: Pauses a running node in a lab.

Creates temporary SSH access to a lab node and returns connection details for an external SSH client.
This endpoint does not create a browser terminal session. For browser terminals, use `/api/v1/labs/{labName}/nodes/{nodeName}/terminal-sessions` with `protocol=ssh`.

parameters.description: Full container name of the node (e.g., clab-my-lab-srl1)
parameters.name: nodeName

post.description: Starts a stopped node in a lab.
Stops a running node in a lab.

Creates a constrained interactive terminal session for an owned lab node using one of the supported protocols: `ssh`, `shell`, or `telnet`.

**Protocol behavior**
- `ssh`: launches the server-side `ssh` client in a PTY directly to the node management IP and streams that terminal over WebSocket.
- `shell`: launches a PTY-backed `docker|podman exec -it <container> <server-selected-command>` session.
- `telnet`: launches a PTY-backed `docker|podman exec -it <container> telnet 127.0.0.1 <port>` session.

**Security notes**
- The backend chooses the launch command for every protocol. Clients choose only the protocol and terminal size.
- Access is limited to owned nodes unless the caller is a configured superuser.

**Relationship to `/api/v1/labs/{labName}/nodes/{nodeName}/ssh`**
- This endpoint creates the interactive browser terminal session.
- The separate `/ssh` endpoint returns temporary SSH access details for an external SSH client and is not required for browser terminals.

parameters.description: Full container name of the node (e.g., clab-my-lab-srl1)
parameters.name: nodeName

post.description: Unpauses a paused node in a lab.
Saves the running configuration for nodes in a lab.

parameters.description: Save config only for specific nodes
parameters.name: nodeFilter

post.description: Executes SSHX share action (attach, detach, reattach) for a lab.

get.description: Returns annotations for the specified lab. For deployed labs, the running annotations path is preferred and local files are used as fallback.

put.description: Updates annotations for the specified lab. For deployed labs, writes to the running annotations path when present and otherwise writes to local files.

get.description: Streams topology YAML/annotations change events for a single lab topology document pair as NDJSON.

parameters.description: Relative topology YAML or annotations path inside lab directory
parameters.name: path

get.description: Reads a file from within the specified lab directory using a scoped relative path.

parameters.description: Relative file path inside lab directory
parameters.name: path

put.description: Writes a file inside the specified lab directory using a scoped relative path.

parameters.description: Relative file path inside lab directory
parameters.name: path

delete.description: Deletes a file inside the specified lab directory using a scoped relative path.

parameters.description: Relative file path inside lab directory
parameters.name: path

head.description: Checks whether a file exists inside the specified lab directory.

parameters.description: Relative file path inside lab directory
parameters.name: path

post.description: Renames or moves a file inside the specified lab directory using scoped relative paths.

parameters.description: Old and new relative file paths
parameters.name: rename_request

get.description: Returns the topology YAML for the specified lab. For deployed labs, the running topology source path is preferred and local files are used as fallback.

put.description: Updates the topology YAML for the specified lab. For deployed labs, writes to the running topology source when present and otherwise writes to local files.

get.description: Returns the custom TopoViewer icons available for a lab, merging lab-local .clab-icons with the global ~/.clab/icons library. Lab-local icons take precedence.

post.description: Copies used custom icons from the global ~/.clab/icons library into the lab-local .clab-icons directory and removes unused lab-local custom icons.

get.description:
Returns active SSH sessions.

**Notes**
- Regular users see only their sessions.
- Superusers can include all sessions via the `all` query parameter.

parameters.description: If true and user is superuser, shows sessions for all users (default: false)
parameters.name: all

403.description: Forbidden (non-superuser attempting to list all sessions)

delete.description: Terminates a specific SSH session by port.

get.description: Returns metadata and lifecycle state for a terminal session.

delete.description: Terminates a terminal session owned by the caller (or any session for a superuser).

get.description:
Upgrades the request to a WebSocket connection for terminal input, output, resize, and close events.

**Protocol notes**
- Connect with a WebSocket client to this endpoint after creating a terminal session.
- Client messages are JSON objects with `type` set to `input`, `resize`, or `close`.
- Server messages are JSON objects with `type` set to `ready`, `output`, or `exit`.

post.description:
Creates a CA certificate and private key. Requires superuser privileges.

**Notes**
- Files are stored in the user's `~/.clab/certs/<ca_name>/` directory on the server.

Creates a certificate/key and signs it with a previously generated CA. Requires superuser privileges.

**Notes**
- Files are stored in the user's `~/.clab/certs/<ca_name>/` directory.

400.description: Invalid input parameters (name, hosts, CA name, etc.)

post.description: Disables TX checksum offload on the eth0 interface of a container. Requires superuser privileges.

500.description: Internal server error or clab execution failed

post.description: Installs and starts EdgeShark services on the host runtime using compose.

get.description: Returns whether EdgeShark is reachable and its runtime details.

post.description: Stops and removes EdgeShark services managed by compose.
Resets (removes) netem impairments from a specific interface of a containerlab node. Requires superuser privileges.
Sets netem impairments (delay, jitter, loss, rate limiting, corruption) on a specific interface of a containerlab node. Requires superuser privileges.

get.description: Lists netem impairments for a given containerlab node. Requires superuser privileges.

post.description: Creates a virtual Ethernet (vEth) pair between two endpoints (container, host, bridge, or ovs-bridge). Requires superuser privileges.
Creates a VxLAN tunnel interface and sets up tc rules for traffic redirection. Requires superuser privileges.

400.description: Invalid input parameters (remote, link, id, port, etc.)

delete.description: Deletes VxLAN tunnel interfaces that match the provided prefix (default: `vx-`). Requires superuser privileges.

parameters.description: Prefix of VxLAN interfaces to delete
parameters.name: prefix

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's persisted TopoViewer custom node templates. If none have been saved yet, seeded defaults matching VS Code are returned.

put.description: Replaces the authenticated user's full TopoViewer custom node template collection.

post.description: Creates or updates a single TopoViewer custom node template for the authenticated user.
Sets the default TopoViewer custom node template for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Deletes a single TopoViewer custom node template for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's global custom TopoViewer icon library from ~/.clab/icons.

post.description: Uploads a new global custom TopoViewer icon into ~/.clab/icons for the authenticated user.

409.description: Rejected due to built-in icon name conflict

delete.description: Deletes a global custom TopoViewer icon from ~/.clab/icons for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns a list of system users. Requires superuser privileges.

post.description: Creates a new system user. Requires superuser privileges.

get.description: Returns details for a specific user. Requires superuser privileges or the user's own account.

403.description: Forbidden (Not superuser or not the user's own account)

put.description: Updates an existing user. Requires superuser privileges or the user's own account.

403.description: Forbidden (Not superuser or not the user's own account)

delete.description: Deletes a user from the system. Requires superuser privileges.

put.description: Changes a user's password. Requires superuser privileges or the user's own account.

403.description: Forbidden (Not superuser or not the user's own account)

get.description: Returns version information for the containerlab library in use.
Checks whether a newer containerlab release is available.
Returns basic health status for the API server.

post.description: Authenticates a user and returns a JWT token.

caName.description: Name of the CA cert/key files (without .pem/.key) previously generated

hosts.description: SANs (DNS names or IPs), comma-separated in clab, array here

group.description: Group assigned in topology (Might not always be present)

labPath.description: Path to the topology file used (relative)

lab_name.description: Name of the lab this node belongs to (redundant with map key but present)

owner.description: OS user from clab inspect output (Used for authorization)

cmd.description: The command and its arguments as executed

return-code.description: Exit code of the command inside the container

topologySourceUrl.description:
Option 2: Remote Topology Source URL.
Provide a URL to a Git repository, a specific .clab.yml file in Git (github/gitlab), or a raw HTTP(S) URL.
If this is provided, 'topologyContent' MUST be empty.

error.example: Invalid credentials or user not in allowed group

lab.description: Lab name this event belongs to

attributes.description: Event attributes containing details about the event

time.description: Unix timestamp when the event occurred

count.description: Number of nodes in this tier

deployOutput.description:
The output from the deploy command (only if Deploy=true). JSON object keyed by lab name.
Use swaggertype:"object" to represent json.RawMessage in Swagger.

savedFilePath.description: Path where the file was saved (if Deploy=true, it's the path in the user's ~/.clab dir; if Deploy=false, it's the OutputFile path if provided).

topologyYaml.description: The generated topology YAML (only if Deploy=false and OutputFile is empty).

corruption.description: Percentage (might be missing in older clab versions)

containerName.description: Container/node name to reset impairments on (e.g., "clab-my-lab-srl1").

interface.description: Interface name or interface alias to reset impairments on (e.g., "eth1" or "mgmt0").

containerName.description: Container/node name to apply impairments on (e.g., "clab-my-lab-srl1").

interface.description: Interface name or interface alias to apply impairments on (e.g., "eth1" or "mgmt0").

duration.description: How long the access should be valid for (e.g., "1h", "30m")

sshUsername.description: Optional override for container's SSH user

output.description: Detailed output from the 'clab save' command (often from stderr).

cols.description: Cols is the requested terminal width in characters.

protocol.description: Protocol selects the terminal transport: ssh, shell, or telnet.

rows.description: Rows is the requested terminal height in characters.

sshUsername.description: SSHUsername optionally overrides the SSH username for protocol=ssh.

telnetPort.description: TelnetPort optionally overrides the telnet destination port for protocol=telnet.

deploymentState.description: undeployed (runtime state is derived from events stream)

checkResult.description: Raw output string from 'clab version check'.
checkResult.example:
🎉 A newer containerlab version (0.62.2) is available!
Release notes: https://containerlab.dev/rn/0.62/#0622
Run 'sudo clab version upgrade' or see https://containerlab.dev/install/ for installation options.

versionInfo.example:
version: 0.50.0
commit: 1234abcd
date: 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z
github: https://github.com/srl-labs/containerlab
release notes: https://containerlab.dev/rn/0.50/

aEndpoint.description:
Endpoint A definition. Format: <node-name>:<interface-name> OR <kind>:<node-name>:<interface-name>
Example: "clab-demo-node1:eth1" or "bridge:br-1:br-eth1" or "host:veth-eth1"

bEndpoint.description:
Endpoint B definition. Format: <node-name>:<interface-name> OR <kind>:<node-name>:<interface-name>
Example: "clab-demo-node2:eth1" or "ovs-bridge:ovsbr-1:br-eth1"

mtu.description: MTU for the vEth pair. Defaults to 9500 if omitted.

dev.description: Optional: Linux device to use for the tunnel source. Auto-detected if omitted.

id.description: VxLAN Network Identifier (VNI). Defaults to 10 if omitted.

link.description: Name of the existing interface in the root namespace to bridge traffic with.

mtu.description: Optional: MTU for the VxLAN interface. Auto-calculated if omitted.

port.description: UDP port number for the VxLAN tunnel. Defaults to 14789 if omitted.

BearerAuth.description: Type "Bearer" followed by a space and JWT token. Example: "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..."
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
